observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the servicing bank for the submission of
debit and credit advice or any other substitute documents that would support
their recording of the unrecorded debit and credit memo in the books;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to arrive
at a correct cash balance every recording period; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and all concerned personnel to refrain from using the cash
balances of the 20 per cent DF in the settlement of other expenses of the
Municipality and to use the fund only for the purpose for which it was created.
Moreover, prioritize the immediate refund of the utilized/borrowed funds from the
20 per cent DF and correct the bank balance of the DRRMF to reflect the funds’
unexpended balance, pursuant to existing budgeting rules and regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the:
a. Property/Supplies custodians to conduct physical count of inventories semi-
annually, and to prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each
year; and reconcile the results of the actual physical count balances with the
records of the Accounting Office; and
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated supplies ledger cards for each
commodity/stock pursuant to Chapter 7, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count of all
properties of the Municipality and thereafter reconcile the Report of Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the accounting
records; Should reconciliation of the RPCPPE and accounting records
appeared to still be highly difficult or improbable, we recommend that the
Municipality avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE afforded under COA
Circular No. 2020-006; and
a. the General Services Office to renew and update the ARE together with the
property numbers of all PPEs, consistent with the provisions of MNGAS for
LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to comply with RCALGU in recording and classifying
the advances granted by the Municipality; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of bond of accountable officers
who were granted large amount of cash advances pursuant to Treasury
Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009, otherwise, limit the amount of their
cash advances up to the maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bond.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the
registration and transfer of the title of the said parcel of land from the previous
owners to the Municipality of Naic;
b. the Municipal Accountant to investigate the discrepancy of P14,026.00 between the
list of land prepared by the Accounting Office and the recorded amount in the
Financial Statement to facilitate reconciliation with the other records of land; and
c. the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to reconcile the discrepancy of
P4,150,668.40 between their records to come up with the correct value of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to:
a. renew the registration with the LTO of the 10 vehicles owned by the Municipality
in compliance with Section 5 of Batas Pambansa Bilang 74 and follow-up the
number plates of the six vehicles; and
b. prepare the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties for
all unserviceable government vehicles with total book value of P486,268.06
together with other required documents on disposal for submission the COA
Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
c. BPLO to require concerned suppliers to provide written explanation, copy
furnished the COA Audit Team, why they did not fully report their gross sales for
purpose of computation of their business taxes and to institute appropriate action
against erring suppliers, if warranted;
d. BPLO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to use the information
gathered from the list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted
with the Municipality and barangays in the preceding calendar year to serve as
one of the bases in the determination of income levels of various business
establishments for purpose of calculating local business tax; and
e. Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of underpayments of business taxes
totaling P357,833.13 including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to
the rates provided in the Municipal Revenue Code, otherwise Notice of Charge will
be issued by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with his Co-Chairman in the
LSB and other members of the Local School Board:
a. review and approve the SEF Budget for the ensuing year on or before October
31 of the current year; and
b. regularly monitor and evaluate the balance of the SEF to determine the actual
unexpended balance thereof and ensure its inclusion in the SEF budget in the
ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the LSB submit valid justification to
the COA Audit Team why the amount of P2,569,493.53 was charged against the SEF
although the pertinent expenses were not allowed to be charged thereto or
alternatively cause the restoration of the amount using the General Fund subject to
the Municipality’s compliance with existing budgeting rules and regulations,
otherwise the amount will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, the
Municipal Accountant and the OIC-MDRRMO to refrain from utilizing the
unexpended balances of the LDRRMF unless the projects or programs to be
financed thereby are specified under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP and the
corresponding Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are prepared and/or approved by the
Sangguniang Bayan through an ordinance.

We also recommended that he require the OIC-MDRRMO to submit the monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end
of each month to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly
monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs in the GPB to ensure that the gender
issues identified therein are correspondingly addressed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and all
concerned personnel to refrain from using the Trust Fund in the settlement of other
expenses of the Municipality and to use the Trust Fund only for the purpose for which
it was created. Moreover, prioritize the immediate refund of the utilized/borrowed
funds from the Trust Funds pursuant to existing budgeting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. GSO to closely collaborate with the accounting Municipal Accountant to ensure that the RPCPPE is reconciled with the accounting records of PPE; and
b. Municipal Accountant to make sure that the balances of the PPE in the Statement of Financial Position are equal or reconciled with the PPELC balances.
observations.managementAction: records were still unreconciled at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to review the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and make sure that their balances are always equal at the end of the year;
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that set-up of collections from RPT/SET Receivables are properly recorded in the books pursuant to Sections 20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs); and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate other causes of the net deficiencies of P22,727,962.66 between their records and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balances of the subject receivables.
observations.managementAction: Records were tallied for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Property/Supplies custodians to conduct physical count of inventories semi-annually, and to prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and reconcile the results of the actual physical count balances with the records of the Accounting Office; and
b. Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated supplies ledger cards for each commodity/stock pursuant to Chapter 7, MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted the RPCI for the Office Supplies Inventory. The GSO submitted the RPCI for the Office Supplies Inventory. The supplies ledger cards were prepared by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
Not all Property Custodians of Supplies submitted their RPCI for the year.
Supplies ledger cards were prepared but not updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Revert the amount of P769,113.37 from Other Payables to Other Payables unappropriated surplus and henceforth, refrain from recording liabilities unless they are supported with complete documentation and/or valid claims, in conformity with Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Commission on Audit Circular No. 76-45; and
b. Prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to classify the liabilities to their respective correct accounts.
observations.managementAction: Payables were reverted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and to make the necessary adjustments to correct the balances;
b. Adjust the funds received from other government accounts amounting to P2,168,293.13 to their proper accounts; and
c. Identify the nature, source and purpose of the funds received and/or collected amounting to P338,067.00 to facilitate proper recording or reclassification.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Market Supervisor and Grand Terminal Supervisors and Municipal Treasurer to consistently send demand/collection letters to delinquent stallholders, and to recommend to proper Municipal authorities the termination of contracts of those who continuously refuse to comply with the demand for payments; and
b. Personnel in-charge on collection of stall rentals to apply for bond in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to delinquent stallholders. The collector was already bonded for the year.
observations.reason: Some stalls were non-operating. There were still delinquent stall holders who were allowed to occupy the stalls despite non-payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to observe the timely submission of financial statements with supporting schedules and other monthly financial reports;
b) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the complete books of final entry to facilitate the audit of Municipal accounts; and
c) General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the reports on physical count of inventories not later than January 31, and of property, plant and equipment not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Financial Statements were submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Books of final entry were submitted upon request of the Audit Team. Inventory Reports/Inventory Reports were submitted by the GSO.
observations.reason: Monthly Financial Statements and schedules were not submitted on time. General and Subsidiary Ledgers were not submitted on time. Inventory reports were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MSWDO to submit justification to the COA Audit Team on why nine Municipal job order personnel were included as ESP-SAP beneficiaries when they were expressly excluded under Item VI-B of DSWD MC No.09, series of 2020.
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MSWDO to submit to the COA the relevant certifications, the properly identified or labeled photos of the beneficiaries and photocopies of their identification cards with specimen signatures required under Items VII(A.1) and VIII(B.4) of DSWD MC No. 09, series of 2020 or valid justifications, for evaluation of the Audit Team, on why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Justifications were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the corrected Summary of COVID-19 Fund Sources and Utilization Report ensuring that the amounts reported therein are accurate and could be validated or counter checked against the supporting disbursement vouchers and schedules.
observations.managementAction: The Report was submitted to the Audit Team for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team the 17 disbursement vouchers totalling the amount and the distribution lists and acknowledgement receipts duly signed by the recipients of food packs and meal supplies distributed by the Municipality; and also draw journal entry voucher to correct the erroneous recording of property, plant and equipment to expense account in the amount of P205,000.00 and comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006 and RCALGU in recording the food and welfare supplies procured and/or expended by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team. The amount was adjusted for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to submit the following to the Audit Team for further evaluation:
a. Office/Travel Orders or any equivalent document from the Municipal Mayor showing the authority of the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19 hazard pay to report for work during the ECQ and Modified ECQ; and
b. Duly approved Daily Time Records (DTRs) of all personnel during the months they were entitled to COVID-19 hazard pay.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a disposal team to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: The GSO was preparing the needed documentation for the disposal.
observations.reason: Disposals were still not yet conducted for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. GSO to closely collaborate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the
RPCPPE is reconciled with the accounting records of PPE; and
b. Municipal Accountant to make sure that the balances of the PPE in the
Statement of Financial Position are equal or reconciled with the PPELC
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to review the RPT and SET Receivables accounts and
make sure that their balances are always equal at the end of the year;
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that set-up of and collections from RPT/SET
Receivables are properly recorded in the books pursuant to Sections 20,
Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs); and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate
other causes of the net deficiencies of P22,727,962.66 between their records
and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balances of the
subject receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. GSO to conduct complete physical count of inventories every semester and
reconcile the results thereof or the Report of Physical Count of Inventories
(RCPI) with the records of the Accounting Office;
b. GSO to submit the RPCI to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year; and
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each
commodity/stock.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Revert the amount of P769,113.37 from Other Payables to unappropriated
surplus and henceforth, refrain from recording liabilities unless they are
supported with complete documentation and/or valid claims, in conformity
with Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Commission on Audit
Circular No. 76-45; and
b. Prepare and draw the necessary adjusting entries to classify the liabilities to
their respective correct accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the
Due to LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts
in the books of the Province and to make the necessary adjustments to
correct the balances;
b. Adjust the funds received from other government agencies amounting to
P2,168,293.13 to their proper accounts; and
c. Identify the nature, source and purpose of the funds received and/or
collected by the Municipality amounting to P338,067.00 to facilitate proper
recording or reclassification.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require
the:
a. Market Supervisor and Grand Terminal Supervisor to strictly enforce the
provisions of the contract of lease of market stalls and the pertinent revenue
code of the Municipality, especially on payments of rentals and the
revocation of contracts and eviction of delinquent stallholders;
b. Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary Journal Entry Voucher based on
the submitted schedule of delinquent accounts in compliance with Section
112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 7 of the International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1, Volume I;
c. Market and Grand Terminal Supervisors and Municipal Treasurer to
consistently send demand/collection letters to delinquent Stallholders, and
to recommend to proper Municipal authorities the termination of contracts of
those who continuously refuse to comply with the demand for payments; and
d. Personnel in-charge on collection of stall rentals to apply for bond in
compliance with Treasury Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to observe the timely submission of financial
statements with supporting schedules and other monthly financial reports;
b. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the complete
books of final entry to facilitate the audit of Municipal accounts; and
c. General Services Officer (GSO) to submit the reports on physical count of
inventories not later than January 31, and of property, plant and equipment
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MSWDO to submit justification to the COA Audit Team on why nine
Municipal job order personnel were included as ESP-SAP beneficiaries
when they were expressly excluded under Item VI-B of DSWD MC No.09,
series of 2020.
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MSWDO to submit to the
COA the relevant certifications, the properly identified or labeled photos of
the beneficiaries and photocopies of their identification cards with
specimen signatures required under Items VII(A.1) and VIII(B.4) of DSWD
MC No. 09, series of 2020 or valid justifications, for evaluation of the Audit
Team, on why the documents could not be produced, otherwise the
transactions will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team the corrected Summary of COVID-19 Fund Sources and
Utilization Report ensuring that the amounts reported therein are accurate and
could be validated or counter checked against the supporting disbursement
vouchers and schedules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
other concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team the 17 disbursement
vouchers totalling P11,746,429.95, together with all supporting documents, and the
distribution lists and acknowledgement receipts duly signed by the recipients of
food packs and meal supplies distributed by the Municipality amounting to
P42,113,710.00, otherwise, these transactions will be suspended in audit.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw journal entry voucher
to correct the erroneous recording of property, plant and equipment to expense
account in the amount of P205,000.00 and comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006
and RCALGU in recording the food and welfare supplies procured and/or
expended by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to submit the
following to the Audit Team for further evaluation:
a. Office/Travel Orders or any equivalent document from the Municipal Mayor
showing the authority of the personnel/workers who claimed COVID-19
hazard pay to report for work during the ECQ and Modified ECQ; and
b. Duly approved Daily Time Records (DTRs) of all personnel during the months
they were entitled to COVID-19 hazard pay.

observations.managementAction:
a. Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 2, Part II of this report.
b. Correcting entries were Implemented
c. Correcting entries were Implemented
d. Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 2, Part II of this report.
e. Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 2, Part II of this report.

Not Reiterated in Implemented Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.
Physical inventory was conducted.

Inventory reports were prepared and submitted this reports.
Stock cards are being maintained.
Inventory reports were prepared and submitted this report.

observations.recommendation: the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due from LGU account of the Municipality with the corresponding reciprocal account in the books of the Province and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Collections made by the Province in behalf of the Municipality of Naic were properly recorded

observations.recommendation: the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to facilitate the preparation of a complete Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) covering current year LDRRMF and unexpended balances from the previous years to utilize and deliver more benefits to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) was properly prepared

a. Schedule of delinquent stallholders were submitted to the Accounting Office
b. Pass the necessary Journal Entry Voucher based on the submitted schedule of delinquent accounts
c. Demand letters were issued to delinquent stallholders
d. Personnel in-charge on collection of stall rentals to apply for bond
e. Special accounts in the general fund for the operation of public market and Grand Terminal are being considered

observations.recommendation: require the immediate bonding of the General Services Officer and all Inventory Custodians in compliance with the provisions of Section 101 of PD 1445 and Sec. 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Accountable officers are properly bonded in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

observations.recommendation: the Municipal Mayor create a disposal team to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: Documents for disposal were being prepared

observations.recommendation:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and restoration of the accounts payable in accordance with the pro-forma entries prescribed under Section 61(6) of the MNGAS, Volume I; and
b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the checks on the existence of their checks at least one month prior to the date when the checks issued become stale as provided by Section 59 of the MNGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already made and the Municipality has no stale checks existence.

observations.recommendation: the Municipal Mayor to instruct the GAD Focal Person, TWG and other concerned officials to thoroughly study and resolve the issues and challenges and consider all risks and other factors that may hinder the full implementation of all programs, projects and activities prior to their inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that they are implemented as planned.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Fund was 61 per cent utilized for CY 2020

observations.recommendation:
a. the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance and to designate another employee as Special Disbursing Officer if it is necessary to make additional cash advances for special activities/projects;
b. concerned officials and employees to ensure that all cash advances granted to them are liquidated within the prescribed period. Further, we recommend that he requires the Municipal Accountant to regularly send demand letters to officers and employees who would fail to liquidate their advances on due dates with a warning that their salaries would be withheld should they still fail to liquidate after demand.
observations.managementAction: Advances were still granted to accountable officers despite outstanding advances at year end.
observations.reason: There were still outstanding advances at year end

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the computation and recording
of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables set up
and the proper recognition of fines and penalties on property taxes are
in accordance with the provisions of Sections 20 and 23, Chapter 3,
Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units and Sections 233 and 235 of Republic Act (RA)
7160;
b. the Municipal Accountant to rectify and reconstruct the incorrect
entries made in CY 2019 on the set up of Real Property Taxes (RPT) and
Special Education Taxes (SET) as guided by the correcting entries
recommended by the Audit Team provided in Appendix 7;
c. the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in
the preparation of the necessary adjusting journal entries to
exclude/remove the fines and penalties previously recorded as RPT and
SET receivables and to identify the unrecorded collections from the
PTO to arrive at the reconciled balances of the RPT and SET
receivables;
d. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO) in securing the subsidiary
ledger of all the Land Tax Dues collected from previous years up to the
current year to update and record the RPT and SET collected in the
books of the Municipality; and
e. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately
coordinate with the PTO whenever the remittances of the latter vary
with the records of the Municipality, in order that timely adjustments or
corrections of affected could be effected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reported balances in the
Statement of Financial Position with the balances recorded in lapsing
schedules;
b. the General Services Officer and the Inventory Committee to conduct
complete physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to
continue exerting efforts to reconcile the variances in their records.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:

a. require all Inventory Custodian to prepare and submit monthly report
of inventories to the Accounting Office for proper recording of
expenses and balances;
b. require all Inventory Custodian and the Accounting Department to
maintain supply stock cards and supply ledger cards, respectively, to
account the receipt and disposition of inventory items and to facilitate
reconciliation of both records; and
c. require the conduct of physical count of all inventory accounts and
submit to the Audit Team the Report of Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) to establish accuracy, validity and existence of the recorded
Inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due
from LGU account of the Municipality with the corresponding reciprocal account
in the books of the Province and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant request the
Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers
showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to
serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as
provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to facilitate the preparation of a
complete Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) covering current year LDRRMF and unexpended balances from the
previous years to utilize and deliver more benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the Market Supervisor and Grand Terminal Supervisor to strictly enforce the
provisions of the contract of lease of market stalls and the pertinent
revenue code of the Municipality, especially on payments of rentals and the
revocation of contracts and eviction of delinquent stallholders;
b. the Market and Grand Terminal Supervisors or Municipal Treasurer to
submit the Schedule of Stallholders with delinquent accounts to the
Municipal Accountant;
c. the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary Journal Entry Voucher
based on the submitted schedule of delinquent accounts in compliance
with Section 112 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 7 of the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 1, Volume I;
d. the Market and Grand Terminal Supervisors and Municipal Treasurer to
consistently send demand/collection letters to delinquent Stallholders,
and to recommend to proper Municipal authorities the termination of
contracts of those who continuously refuse to comply with the demand
for payments;
e. the Market and Grand terminal Supervisors together with their 11 market
collectors to apply for bond in compliance with the Treasury Circular No.
2009-002; and
f. the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the general
fund for the operation of public market in compliance with Section 313 of
Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 1.3.1 of the Manual on Setting up and
Operation of Local Economic Enterprise.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate bonding of the
General Services Officer and all Inventory Custodians in compliance with the
provisions of Section 101 of PD 1445 and Sec. 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-
2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a disposal team to facilitate
the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the
determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, to prevent further
deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and restoration of the
accounts payable in accordance with the pro-forma entries prescribed under
Section 61(6) of the MNGAS, Volume I; and

b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the
existence of their checks at least one month prior to the date when the
checks issued become stale as provided by Section 59 of the MNGAS,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the GAD Focal Person,
TWG and other concerned officials to thoroughly study and resolve the issues
and challenges and consider all risks and other factors that may hinder the full
implementation of all programs, projects and activities prior to their inclusion in
the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that they are implemented as planned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a. the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to
any official or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance
and to designate another employee as Special Disbursing Officer if it is
necessary to make additional cash advances for special
activities/projects; and

b. concerned officials and employees to ensure that all cash advances
granted to them are liquidated within the prescribed period. Further, we
recommend that he requires the Municipal Accountant to regularly send
demand letters to officers and employees who would fail to liquidate their
advances on due dates with a warning that their salaries would be withheld
should they still fail to liquidate after demand.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in reconciling the variances to establish the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management is still searching for some documents that could be used as basis for reconciliation. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2 of this report.
observations.reason: Lack or incomplete records hinder the completion of the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2019. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the certified list which was used in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2019.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully reconcile the variances on RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and these accounts will be for verification. They assured that necessary adjustments will be made once confirmation is made with the Province.
observations.reason: has yet to fully reconcile the variances on Due to/Due from account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to ensure that previous obligations with the Municipality have been paid before issuance/renewal of the business permit for the current year to enforce settlement of their obligations; b. The Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the preceding year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts; c. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/ OIC-BPLO a list of the payments made to Municipality’s contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and d. The Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO to furnish the Municipal Accountant list of unpaid business tax for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) ensured that previous obligations with the Municipality have been settled before issuance of/renewal of business permit. The BPLO will ensure that business taxpayers submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the preceding year and other pertinent records/information. The Municipal Accountant was furnished a copy of list of payments to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding year for the office of the BPLO. The Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO furnished the Municipal Accountant list of unpaid business tax as basis for recording in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balance of trust receipts amounting to P403,393.09 to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal has remitted the unexpended balance of trust receipts amounting to P403,393.09 to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. All concerned officials to strictly comply with the proper out and approval of Driver’s Trip Ticket and issue Reports on fuel consumption for monitoring of fuel consumption of Government Vehicles; b. The Head, General Services Office to formulate and revisit existing fuel utilization policies of the Municipality on fuel utilization, if there was any; c. The BAC to subject the procurement of POL needed in the operations of the Municipality to competitive public bidding or any alternative methods of procurement as maybe applicable under the circumstances, as provided under Section 11 of the Revised Guidelines on the Procurement of Petroleum, Oil and Lubricant (POL) Products.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is currently formulating and monitoring the proper use of motor vehicles and revisit existing fuel utilization policies. The BAC has subjected the procurement of POL to alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: that the Municipal Budget Officer to, henceforth, refrain from obligating and charging LDRRMF expenditures which are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk management activities and conform with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. Administrative expenses to be charged to the respective appropriations of the Office concerned funded out of the General Fund as provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant are monitoring the charges against the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. The Municipal Disaster Risk Management Council to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF using the sample format illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. All concerned officials to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and c. The MDRRMO be required to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of Fund.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, Budget Office and the Municipal Accountant is focusing on monitoring the LDRRMF. The OIC, Budget Office has submitted the reports but not on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: be required to submit the Monthly Report regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the 10 per cent retention up to 50 per cent of the total contract cost for every project made to contractors prior to any deduction from every progress billing made by the contractor, in conformity with Section 6, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Retention money was already deducted up to 50 per cent of every project completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Treasurer to limit the grant of cash advances to the Disbursing Officer within the allowable maximum cash accountability covered by the bond pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. When there is a need to increase her cash accountability, additional bond should be applied for. On the other hand, ensure the constant use of automated payroll system on the releases of salaries and benefits for safe and better control over handling of cash.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursing Officer’s fidelity bond was increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the reported Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable (RPT/SET Receivable) as of year-end. 2. the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her records with that of the Assessor’s Office to determine ownership and completeness of recorded properties and thereafter do the necessary updating.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant again based the RPT and SET Receivables on real property assessments prepared by the Municipal Assessor due to delayed receipt of report from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Reconciliation of records with that of the Assessor’s Office was undertaken.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records with that of the Assessor’s Office was undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the proceeding year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts; b. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/BPLO a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and c. The Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the three sample contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totalling P59,370.61 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the 2014 Revised Revenue Measures of the Municipality of Naic. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean issuance of corresponding Notice of Charge. We also recommended that the Municipality consider the adoption of the provisions of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95 in the revision of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and the OIC-BPLO assured compliance with the audit recommendations. The Municipal Mayor also informed that since the Municipality’s Revenue Code is for revision in CY 2019 the provisions of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95 shall be adopted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 40

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in reconciling
the noted variances to establish the reliability of the balances of the PPE
accounts reflected in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require The Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of
the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the
reported Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable
(RPT/SET Receivable) as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to
and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal
accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should request the Provincial Accountant to
furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount
collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in
recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under
Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO)
to ensure that the required business tax in the preceding year have been paid
before issuing/renewing the business permit for the current year to enforce
settlement of their obligations;
b. The Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO to require business taxpayers to submit
Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the
proceeding year and other pertinent records/information for the determination
of their gross receipts;
c. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO a list
of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the
municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the
bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and
d. The Municipal Treasurer/OIC-BPLO to furnish the Municipal Accountant list of
unpaid business tax for recording in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipality adopt the provisions of DOF-Local
Finance Circular No. 3-95 in the revision of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balance of trust receipts totaling
P403,393.09 which has been outstanding for more than two years to the National
Treasury as required under Executive Order No. 431 dated May 30, 2005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. All concerned officials to strictly comply with the proper preparation and
approval of Driver’s Trip Ticket and issue a certification should the travel be
proven to be confidential in nature and prepare reports for monitoring of fuel
consumption as provided in the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of
Government Motor Vehicles;
b. The Head, General Services Office to formulate guidelines on the use of the
motor vehicles and revisit existing policies of the Municipality on fuel
utilization, if there was any; and
c. The BAC to subject the procurement of POL needed in the operations of the
Municipality to competitive public bidding or any alternative methods of
procurement as maybe applicable under the circumstances, as provided under
Section 11 of the Revised Guidelines on the Procurement of Petroleum, Oil and
Lubricant (POL) Products.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC, Municipal Budget
Office to, henceforth, refrain from obligating and charging LDRRMF for
expenditures which are not directly related to the implementation of disaster risk
management activities and conform with the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC,
DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1. Charge administrative
expenses to the respective appropriations of the Office concerned funded out of
the General Fund as provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to identify and
incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF using
the sample format illustrated in COA Circular No. 2012-002;
b. All concerned officials to optimize the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on
programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its capacity to prevent
and mitigate disaster risks; and
c. The MDRRMO be required to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to facilitate
proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
deduct the 10 per cent retention up to 50 per cent of the total contract cost for
each infrastructure project, prior to any deduction from every progress billing
made by the contractor, in conformity with Section 6, Annex E of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Treasurer to limit
the grant of cash advances to the Disbursing Officer within the allowable
maximum cash accountability covered by the bond pursuant to COA Circular
No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009. When there is a need to increase
her cash accountability, additional bond should be applied for. On the other hand,
ensure the constant use of automated payroll system on the releases of salaries
and benefits for safe and better control over handling of cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in reconciling the noted variances to establish the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Investment Property accounts have been reconciled with that records as of year-end of CY 2018. Continuous effort is being done by the Accounting and General Services Offices.
observations.reason: Lack or incomplete records hinder the completion of the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to: a. reclassify the road lots from Land account (1-07-01-010) to Infrastructure Assets – Road Network (1-07-03-010) account in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008; b. drop from the Land account and the lot owned by the Provincial Government of Cavite with assessed value of P10,800.00 erroneously recorded in the books of accounts; c. adjust the recorded value of land and road lots based on acquisition cost and/or fair value as provided under PPSAS 17 for fair presentation of both accounts in the financial statements; and d. request the assistance of the appraisal committee in establishing the fair value of the two donated lots at the time of donation for proper recognition in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Road lots have been reclassified to its proper account Infrastructure Assets – Road Network (1-07-03-010) account in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008; The value of recorded lots has been adjusted based on acquisition cost and/or fair value.
observations.reason: No Appraisal Committee was constituted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and Cavite Provincial Treasurer’s Office to determine the amount of taxes due and the possibility of entering into compromise agreement as to payment of penalties and surcharges; b. the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure that the Deed of Sale provides for the payment of taxes and other expenses related thereto and that all other pertinent documentary requirements for the titling of lands are promptly processed and submitted to the BIR and Register of Deeds, respectively; c. the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality; and d. the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer to facilitate the immediate transfer of title of the 4 procured lots and 2 donated lots in the name of the Municipality. We also recommended that verification as to the timeliness in the payment of taxes and other related expenses be made in order to avoid penalties and charges. In case of delays and incurrence of penalties and charges due to late payments, responsible officials/employees shall be personally held liable and be made to pay the same on their personal account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer conferred with the BIR and she was informed of the documents needed. The Municipal Fund was set aside for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality. The Municipal Assessor has informed the documents needed which prevented the immediate transfer of lots in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
No reported action was taken in CY 2018
Lack of documents prevented the immediate transfer.
Officials responsible for verification as to the timeliness in the payment of taxes and other related expenses are no longer connected with the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record the payment for partial accomplishments of on-going infrastructure projects to Construction-in-Progress account and debit the appropriate accumulated asset account only upon completion and acceptance of the asset, only when the asset is already available for use, in conformity with PAG3 and PAG5 on PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Affected accounts were adjusted and transferred to Construction-in-Progress account. Corresponding adjustment to accumulated depreciation was also made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the establishment of the GAD Database to ensure that GAD formulated policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality and facilitate the monitoring of the improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women within the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The establishment of data base is in process with the on-going implementation of Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS)
observations.reason: CBMS is still on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that maybe headed by the Municipal Accountant to examine what could be the possible causes for the non-attainment of the target revenues of the NGCT project by comparing the conditions and the feasibility study with the actual situation and outcomes and appropriately address them in order to make the project viable and adequately or earn more than enough to pay off the loan used to finance the implementation of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality came out with a plan to transfer the Terminal to the adjacent lot presently occupied by wet section of the public market which is more accessible and spacious. The existing terminal will then be occupied by the wet section of the public market.
observations.reason: Construction/conversion of facilities is now on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to: a. reclassify the road lots from Land account (1-07-01-010) to Infrastructure Assets - Road Network (1-07-03-010) account in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008; b. drop from the Land account and the RPCPPE the lot owned by the Provincial Government of Cavite with assessed value of P10,800.00 erroneously recorded in the books of accounts of the Municipality; c. adjust the recorded value of land and road lots based on acquisition cost and/or fair value as provided under PPSAS 17 for fair presentation of both accounts in the financial statements; and d. request the assistance of the appraisal committee in establishing the fair value of the two donated lots at the time of donation for proper recognition in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and General Services Offices are still in the process of reconciling their respective records, hence, actions on recommendations were put on hold.
observations.reason: The Accounting and General Services Offices are still in the process of reconciling their respective records, hence, actions on recommendations were put on hold.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in
reconciling the noted variances to establish the reliability of the balances of the
PPE accounts reflected in the financial statements.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant informed of the on-going verification and
reconciliation of records being undertaken by both the Accounting and General
Services Offices.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
a. reclassify the road lots from Land account (1-07-01-010) to
Infrastructure Assets - Road Network (1-07-03-010) account in
conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008;
b. drop from the Land account and the RPCPPE the lot owned by the
Provincial Government of Cavite with assessed value of P10,800.00
erroneously recorded in the books of accounts of the Municipality;
c. adjust the recorded value of land and road lots based on acquisition
cost and/or fair value as provided under PPSAS 17 for fair
presentation of both accounts in the financial statements; and
d. request the assistance of the appraisal committee in establishing the
fair value of the two donated lots at the time of donation for proper
recognition in the books of accounts.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant assured that adjustments in the books of accounts
will be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
1. the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Treasurer to strictly comply with
the provisions of Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs,
Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the reported Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable
(RPT/SET Receivable) as of year-end.
2. the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her records with that of
the Assessor’s Office to determine ownership and completeness of
recorded properties and thereafter do the necessary updating.

Management’s Comment:
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor promised to reconcile
their respective records to come up with a Certified List of Taxpayers together with
the amount due and collectible for the year and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Bureau of Internal
Revenue (BIR) and the Cavite Provincial Treasurer’s Office to
determine the amount of taxes due and the possibility of entering into
compromise agreement as to payment of penalties and surcharges;
b. the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure
that the Deed of Sale provides for the payment of taxes and other
expenses related thereto and that all other pertinent documentary
requirements for the titling of lands are promptly processed and
submitted to the BIR and Register of Deeds, respectively;
c. the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the titling of lands
owned by the Municipality;
d. the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer to facilitate the
immediate transfer of title of the 4 procured lots and 2 donated lots
in the name of the Municipality.

We also recommended that verification as to the timeliness in the payment of
taxes and other related expenses be made in order to avoid penalties and
charges. In case of delays and incurrence of penalties and charges due to
late payments, responsible officials/employees shall be personally held liable and
be made to pay the same on their personal account.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Mayor assured compliance with the audit recommendations
and instructed the OIC, Municipal Budget Officer to include in the budget funds for
the titling of land in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to record the payment for partial accomplishments of on-going
infrastructure projects to Construction-in-Progress account and debit the
appropriate asset account only upon completion and acceptance of the same.
Likewise, start the computation of allowance for depreciation and depreciation
expenses using the estimated useful life provided under COA Circular No. 2003-
007, only when the asset is already available for use, in conformity with PAG3
and PAG5 on PPSAS 17.

Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant informed that corresponding adjustments have been
made in the books of accounts thru JEV No. 100-2018-01-07615 dated January 31,
2018.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing
Office (BPLO) to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn
Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the
proceeding year and other pertinent records/information for the
determination of their gross receipts;
b. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal
Treasurer/BPLO a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers
who transacted business with the municipal government in the
preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment
of business taxes in the current year; and
c. The Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the three
sample contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under
collection totaling P59,370.61 and enforce the collection of
underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable,
pursuant to the rates provided in the 2014 Revised Revenue Measures
of the Municipality of Naic. Absence of documented action or
information to counter the under collection shall mean issuance of
corresponding Notice of Charge.

We also recommended the Municipality to consider the adoption of the
provisions of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95 in the revision of the
Municipality’s Revenue Code.

Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant reported that she started furnishing the Business
and Licensing Office with list of suppliers/contractors who transacted business with
the Municipality and the corresponding payments made to them. The Municipal
Mayor agreed to consider the adoption of the provisions of DOF-Local Finance
Circular No. 3-95 in the revision of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the
establishment of the GAD Database to ensure that GAD formulated policies,
programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues
and concerns within the Municipality and facilitate the monitoring of the
improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women within the
Municipality.

Management’s Comment:
The MDPC explained that that the GAD Database build-up will now be
facilitated with the implementation of the Community Base Monitoring System
(CBMS). An orientation has already been conducted by the DILG focal person in
CBMS to the different offices of the Municipality. It is expected that the data that
will be generated from the project will address not only the need for GAD database
but also supply necessary data as basis for projects/programs formulation of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee that
maybe headed by the Municipal Accountant to examine what could be the
possible causes for the non-attainment of the target revenues of the NGCT
project by comparing the conditions and projections in the feasibility study with
the actual situation and outcomes and appropriately address them in order to
make the project viable and adequately or earn more than enough to pay off the
loan used to finance the implementation of the same.

Management’s Comment:
The official in charge of the terminal operations informed that due to its
location the terminal could not attract concessionaires who would to do business
within the premises. Moreover, due to small entrance big buses could not be
accommodated, hence the use of the terminal was not maximized. However,
Management is trying to find ways how to make the terminal a productive enterprise.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the reported Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable (RPT/SET Receivable) as of year-end. b. the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her records with that of the Assessor’s Office to determine ownership and completeness of recorded properties and thereafter do the necessary updating.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor promised to reconcile their respective records to come up with a Certified List of Taxpayers together with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The OIC- Treasurer will still coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for comparison of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all assets owned by the Municipality to ascertain their existence; b. the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to continue their efforts in reconciling their respective records to establish the reliability of the balances of the PPE accounts reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: On-going physical inventory. Reconciliation of Accounting and General Services Offices is still in process.
observations.reason: The physical inventory was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to: a. exert extra effort in retrieving documents/information that will support and facilitate the computation of the depreciation expenses for PPE not subjected to depreciation. However, if effort prove to be futile, create an appraisal committee that will determine the fair value of these assets and the remaining useful life to serve as basis in the computation of depreciation on the aforesaid property for fair presentation in the financial statements; and b. to keep and maintain updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of PPE for reference in the computation of depreciation.
observations.managementAction: PPE has been provided with depreciation. The Accounting Office has provided PPELC for PPE owned by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province, and make the necessary adjustments. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Provincial Accountant was done. As of year-end of CY 2017 an unadjusted difference of only P23.20 was noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and the Cavite Provincial Treasurer’s Office to determine the amount of taxes due and the possibility of entering into compromise agreement as to payment of penalties and surcharges; b. the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to ensure that the Deed of Sale provides for the payment of taxes and other expenses related thereto and that all other pertinent documentary requirements for the titling of lands are promptly processed and submitted to the BIR and Register of Deeds, respectively; c. the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality; d. the Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer to facilitate the immediate transfer of title of the 4 procured lots and 2 donated lots in the name of the Municipality; and e. the General Services Officer to take custody of the CTCs and other documents pertaining to land owned by the Municipality and be responsible for its safekeeping.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor assured compliance with the audit recommendations and instructed the OIC, Municipal Budget Officer to include in the budget funds for the titling of land in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Transfer of title not yet done. Preparation of necessary documents needed has to be undertaken. Completion of necessary documents still on-going. Procurement of safety vault still not in-process. Personnel involved are no longer connected with the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a demand letter requiring Laviña Geosystems and Surveys the completion of its obligations under the Contract of Service which include, among others, the delivery/ submission of the Subdivision Survey Plan, Lot Descriptions together with the Narrative Technical Descriptions, all duly approved by the Land Management Services of the DENR-Region IVA. We also required the imposition of liquidated damages equivalent to 1/10 of 1% of the cost of unperformed portion of the contract for every day of delay as provided under Section 68 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the needed approved Cadastral Map was obtained by the contractor. However, disparities and encroachment were noted between the Cadastral Map result of initial survey, hence, the PNR, being the landowner, requested an amendment for the boundary of PNR property from the LMS-DENR-Reg.IV. Once approved the final Survey Returns will be submitted to the same Office together with other documents for verification and approval.
observations.reason: Actions from the concerned government agencies are needed before the obligations of the contractor can be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the preceding year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts; b. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/BPLO a list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and c. The Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the two contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling P73,928.69 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean issuance of corresponding Notice of Charge. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor propose to the Sangguniang Bayan to consider the revision of Section 2B.02(a)(7) of the Municipality’s Revenue Code to conform with Section 5 of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has provided the BPLO with list of suppliers/contractors that transacted business with the Municipality together with the payment made to them. The Municipal Mayor agreed to consider the adoption of the provisions of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95 in the revision of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.reason: Revision of the Municipality’s Revenue Code is due in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer submit justification/basis on the computation of the project cost. We also recommended that Section 22 of Revenue Regulation No. 4-2007 issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue be adopted in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has adopted the 5% VAT in the preparation of ABC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to expedite the establishment of the GAD database to ensure that GAD formulated policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality and facilitate the monitoring of the improvement of status or changes in the condition of men and women within the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MDPC explained that the GAD Database build-up will now be facilitated with the implementation of the Community Base Monitoring System (CBMS). An orientation has already been conducted by the DILG focal person in CBMS to the different offices of the Municipality. It is expected that the data that will be generated from the project will address not only the need for GAD database but also supply necessary data as basis for projects/programs formulation of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some projects were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure: a. that payments to contractors are based on the actual work accomplished as certified in the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) by the Municipal Project Engineer; and b. that the overpayment is offset from the final payment to be made to the contractor and strictly adhere to Item 5.1 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 in processing progress payments of the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Payment based on actual accomplishment per certified SWA is strictly observed. The contractor was paid only for the contract cost.
observations.reason: Excess payment was no longer noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of    Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I by ensuring that:
a. the required Certified List of Taxpayers with information on the amount due and collectible therefrom  is submitted by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to the Office of the  Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year to serve as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables; and
b.  the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies be submitted by the OIC, Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year for reconciliation and adjustments of balances  of RPT/SET Receivables at year-end.

We    recommended  that  the  Municipal Mayor   advise  the    Municipal Accountant  to:
exert extra effort in retrieving documents/information or records that will support and facilitate the computation of the depreciation expenses for   PPE not subjected to depreciation.  However, if effort prove to be futile, create an appraisal committee that will determine the fair value of these assets and the remaining useful life to serve as basis in the computation of depreciation on the aforesaid  property for fair presentation  in the financial statements.
to keep and maintain updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each class of PPE  for reference in the computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancy in the Due to and Due from LGU accounts of the Municipality with that of its reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and make the necessary adjustments.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant  should request the Provincial Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as  basis in recording the Due from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We  recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a demand letter requiring Laviña Geosystems  and Surveys the completion of its obligations under the Contract of Service which include, among others, the delivery/submission of the Subdivision Survey Plan, Lot Descriptions together with the Narrative Technical Descriptions, all duly approved by the Land Management Services of the DENR-Region IVA.
