We also recommended the imposition of liquidated damages equivalent to 1/10 of 1%  of the cost of unperformed  portion of the contract for every day of delay as provided under Section 68 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office/Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) to require business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales for all their transactions for the preceding year  and other pertinent records/information for the determination of their gross receipts;
The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer/BPLO a  list of payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted business with the municipal government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; and
The Municipal Treasurer/BPLO assess the gross receipts of the two contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totalling P73,928.69 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean  issuance of  corresponding Notice of Charge.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor  propose to the Sangguniang Bayan to consider  the revision of Section 2B.02(a)(7) of the Municipality’s Revenue Code to conform with Section 5 of DOF-Local Finance Circular No. 3-95.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer  submit justification/basis on the computation of ABC at 10% for evaluation of the Audit Team.  We also recommended that  Section 22 of Revenue Regulation  No. 4-2007 issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue be adopted  in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to the advantage of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development  Coordinator to expedite the establishment of the  GAD database  to ensure that GAD formulated policies, programs, plans and activities are gender-responsive and address gender issues and concerns within the Municipality.
Likewise, we recommended that  the  Focal Point System   maximize the  utilization  of the GAD Budget by ensuring   that  programs, projects and activities in the GAD Plan are fully implemented and addressed the existing GAD issues and concerns within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure:
that payments to contractors are based on the actual work accomplished  provided in the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) as certified by the Municipal/Project Engineer; and
that the overpayment is offset from the final payment to be made to the contractor and strictly adhere to Item 5.1 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 in processing progress payments of the contractors.

difference

P580,285.25

of land, road

buildings are

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  
that the 
Municipality
fully comply
with the basic
requirements of
RA No. 9003, or
the Solid Waste
Management Act
of 2000.

We also
recommended that
the Municipal
Mayor through the
local SWM Board
consider engaging
the services of a
government
accredited solid
waste hauling and
disposal service
provider for the
hauling and transport
of its residual
waste to government
accredited sanitary
landfill for final
disposal.

We likewise
recommended the
conduct of massive
information and
education
campaign (IEC) 
that will increase the
awareness and
cooperation of the
constituents on
proper solid waste
management such as
rules on “no
littering” and the
mandatory waste
segregation at
source, including the
legal consequences for
non-compliance with
the law.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality
engaged the
services of a
hauling company
to transport
residual waste.  
How ever, per ECA
result the
establishment of
MRF in the
barangays were
still not
implemented.

Management has
engaged the
services of a
waste disposal
hauling service
provider.
observations.reason:
The establishment
of MRF in every
barangay could
not be
facilitated due
to lack of space.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  
that the Municipal
Mayor establish an
LDRRM Office and
create/ appoint 
officers/personnel for
the mandatory
positions, in
conformity with
Sections 5 and 6 of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-
CSC Joint
Memorandum
Circular No. 2014-1
who will be
responsible for the
development,
implementation and
coordination of the
Municipality’s
DRRM Programs and
submission of
required reports to
COA and other
monitoring agencies.
observations.managementAction:
An LDRRMO IV was
appointed as head
of the MDDRM
Office.  Two
positions are still
to be filled-up
pending
compliance with
CSC rules on
posting of vacant
positions and the
required
qualification/
training of
applicants for the
positions.
observations.reason:
Recruitment  for
two additional
personnel for
LDRRMO personnel
is still
on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  
that the
Municipal Mayor
strengthen the
existing GDFPS by
setting-up its
structure/composition
(i.e. ExeCom; TWG;
Secretariat) as
provided under
Section 4.1.A of JMC
No. 2013-1 and
define the roles and
responsibilities of
GDFPS members in
accordance with the
provisions of the
JMC.  Likewise, we
recommended that
the GAD database,
through the Municipal
Planning and
Development
Coordinator Office, be
established and
maintained to serve
as basis for gender-
responsive planning,
programming and
policy formulation.
observations.managementAction:
The GFPS was
reconstituted to
conform with the
structure provided
under JMC 2013-01
of the PCW, DILG,DBM
and NEDA.  However,
the required
database was still
not completed/updated
as of the end of
CY 2016.  The
available data were
not consolidated and
updated.
observations.reason:
No specific
person was
assigned to
perform the task.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  
that the        
Municipal Mayor:
request the
Sangguniang
Bayan to pass an
ordinance/
resolution declaring/
classifying  the
Market  and
Grand Central
Terminal as
separate economic
enterprise.

require that the use
of market and
terminal stalls be
covered with a
lease contract/
agreement  in
accordance with
the Revenue Code
of the
Municipality and
the Grand
Central Terminal
Policy of 2013,
respectively, to
identify the stall
owners and
establish their
obligation to the
Municipality; and

require the Market
and Terminal
Supervisors to
maintain
complete records on
the operation of the
Public Market and
Naic Grand
Terminal  such  as
individual records
of stallholders
showing the
amount due and
payments made, and
records of permits
issued vis-avis
actual stall
occupants/stallholders,
and initiate the
imposition of
sanctions in
instances of
non-compliance to
the terms of the
lease
contract/agreement.
observations.managementAction:
No SB Resolution
was passed
declaring the
Public Market and
Grand Terminal as
separate economic
enterprise.

The use of market
stalls are now
covered with
lease contract.
However, the use
of the Grand
Central Terminal
is paid on daily
basis.
observations.reason:
The classification
of the Public Market
and Grand Central
Terminal as
separate
enterprises is
still under study.
Management is
still evaluating the
sustainability of
the two.

Some occupants
refuse to sign
contract
particularly
those who have
been occupying the
stall for a
considerable time.

The BPLO is still
retrieving old
records
pertaining to
long-time
occupants of the
stalls.
